descript.abstract: U.S. Transportation Terminals represents the locations within the United States for transportation terminals such as public transport stops (bus stops, taxi stands, tram stops, and coach stops), railway stations (buildings or boarding platforms where goods and passengers can enter or exit), ferry terminals (access/exit points for ferries), and other significant transportation nodes.
descript.purpose: U.S. Transportation Terminals provides the locations of transportation terminals. All transportation terminals are named and show the codes of the state and county they reside in.

cntinfo.cntinst:
In the United States- Direct all inquiries regarding software/data pricing and consulting services to your local Esri Regional Office. For support, contact Esri by telephone (voice) between 5:00 a.m. and 5:00 p.m. Pacific time, Monday through Friday, by dialing +1-888-377-4575; facsimile (fax) available at +1-909-792-0960; or visit https://support.esri.com/; Esri holidays excluded. Email and chat are available through the Support page on My Esri (https://my.esri.com).
Outside the United States- Direct all inquiries regarding software/data pricing, sales, support, and consulting services to your local Esri Office. This information can be found at https://www.esri.com/en-us/contact#c=us&t=0. For other questions or comments, you may contact Esri headquarters by email, telephone, or fax or write to us.

dataqual.logic: The dataset is converted to file geodatabase (FGDB) format using tools in ArcGIS. The geometry is checked, and if needed repaired.
dataqual.complete: After processing, the dataset is checked for drawing display and number of records and file sizes compared with source materials.

horizpa.horizpar: Horizontal positional accuracy is dependent on geographic area. In most areas, accuracy is between 5 and 25 meters (16 and 82 feet). In some areas, accuracy is off by up to several tens of meters (100 feet or more). In the poorest areas, accuracy is off by up to 100 meters (328 feet).

citeinfo.title: Points of Interest, Administrative Area Order 8

procstep.procdesc: The following steps were performed by Esri: Extracted the Points of Interest dataset from TomTom MultiNet North America. Selected the public transport stop, railway station, and ferry terminal features. Removed all fields except FEATTYP and NAME. Renamed field FEATTYP to MNFC. Added field FEATTYPE and copied its values from MultiNet FEATTYP descriptions. Added field TEXTLENGTH and calculated its values to the length of the text in the NAME field. Added field STCTYFIPS and coded it by overlaying on Administrative Area Order 8 (County or equivalent area) dataset from TomTom MultiNet North America.

altsys.altenc: Explicit elevation coordinate included with horizontal coordinates

enttyp.enttypd: The points represent the locations within the United States for transportation terminals such as public transport stops (bus stops, taxi stands, tram stops, and coach stops), railway stations (buildings or boarding platforms where goods and passengers can enter or exit), ferry terminals (access/exit points for ferries), and other significant transportation nodes.

attrdomv.udom: Sequential unique whole numbers that are automatically generated.

attr.attrdef: The FIPS code (two-digit number) for the state/territory in which the transportation terminal is located combined with the FIPS code (three-digit number) for the county (or equivalent area) in which the transportation terminal is located.

codesetd.codesetn: INCITS 38-2009, (Formerly FIPS 5-2) Codes for the Identification of the States and Equivalent Areas within the United States, Puerto Rico, and the Insular Areas; INCITS 31-2009, (Formerly FIPS 6-4) Codes for the Identification of Counties and Equivalent Areas of the United States, Puerto Rico, and the Insular Areas

attr.attrdef: The official name of the transportation terminal.
The feature type description of the transportation terminal.
The MultiNet feature type code of the transportation terminal.
The length of the text in the NAME field.

cntinfo.cntinst:
In the United States, contact the Esri Sales staff at +1-800-447-9778 or visit https://www.esri.com/en-us/contact#c=us&t=1 for more information about our software and data.
Outside the United States, direct all inquiries to your local Esri Office. This information can be found at https://www.esri.com/en-us/contact#c=us&t=0.

distinfo.distliab: See access and use constraints information.

digtinfo.formcont: To use this data requires software that works with FGDB files.

stdorder.ordering: Data and Maps is available only as part of ArcGIS software.

metainfo.metstdn: FGDC Content Standard for Digital Geospatial Metadata
